{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/257676403?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/manizada", "id": 257676403, "login": "manizada", "node_id": "U_kgDOD1vUcw", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/manizada", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-32934", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary
CoreDNS' DNS-over-QUIC (DoQ) server can be driven into large goroutine and memory growth by a remote client that opens many QUIC streams and stalls after sending only 1 byte. Even with a small configured quic { worker_pool_size ... }, CoreDNS still spawns a goroutine per accepted stream (workers + waiters) and active workers can block indefinitely in io.ReadFull() with no per-stream read deadline, enabling unauthenticated remote DoS via memory exhaustion/OOM-kill.

### Details
CoreDNS' DoQ server uses a global worker pool (streamProcessPool) to limit concurrent stream processing, but when the pool is full it still spawns a goroutine per accepted stream that waits to acquire a worker token: select { case s.streamProcessPool <- ...: go ...; default: go ... wait for token ... } (core/dnsserver/server_quic.go)

Additionally, the DoQ message framing reads are blocking io.ReadFull() calls with no per-stream read deadline: readDOQMessage() reads the 2-byte length prefix and message body via io.ReadFull() (core/dnsserver/server_quic.go)

This allows an attacker to pin all workers by sending 1 byte (so io.ReadFull() blocks waiting for the second byte of the DoQ length prefix), while also creating an unbounded backlog of goroutines waiting for a worker token.

Note: this appears to be a result of an incomplete fix/regression for CVE-2025-47950 (GHSA-cvx7-x8pj-x2gw).

### PoC
1. Adjust COREDNS_BIN in the PoC to point at right path (see the top-level const definitions for tunables as well)
2. Run python3 ./doq-dos-repro.py
3. Expected sample output:
*** Start CoreDNS ***
Corefile: /tmp/vh-f003-doq-mem-regression/Corefile
Log: /tmp/vh-f003-doq-mem-regression/coredns.log

*** Baseline sample (idle) ***
rss_kib=49380 go_goroutines=17

*** Build + run partial-stream flooder ***
go: downloading golang.org/x/net v0.43.0
go: downloading golang.org/x/crypto v0.41.0
go: downloading go.uber.org/mock v0.5.2
go: downloading github.com/stretchr/testify v1.11.1
go: downloading golang.org/x/sys v0.35.0
go: downloading github.com/pmezard/go-difflib v1.0.0
go: downloading github.com/davecgh/go-spew v1.1.1
go: downloading gopkg.in/yaml.v3 v3.0.1

*** Candidate sample (during attack) ***
rss_kib=137968 go_goroutines=15557

*** Flooder output ***
opened conns=60 streams_per_conn=256 total_streams=15360

*** Wrote results ***
/tmp/vh-f003-doq-mem-regression/results.json

*** OK ***
DoQ flood caused goroutine/RSS growth despite worker_pool_size.


### Impact
Unauthenticated remote DoS on an encrypted DNS transport via goroutine/RSS growth leading to OOM-kill/crash and service outage.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00469, "percentile": 0.37502}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-28T22:40:38Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-32934"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-05T20:16:35Z", "published_at": "2026-04-28T22:40:38Z", "references": ["https://github.com/coredns/coredns/security/advisories/GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5", "https://github.com/coredns/coredns/security/advisories/GHSA-cvx7-x8pj-x2gw", "https://github.com/coredns/coredns/releases/tag/v1.14.3", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-32934", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/coredns/coredns/security-advisories/GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/coredns/coredns", "summary": "CoreDNS' DoQ worker pool does not bound stream backlog", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T15:27:59Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.14.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coredns/coredns"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.14.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: CoreDNS' DoQ worker pool does not bound stream backlog
